_note: Slide-deck tour. intro = start lightbox. stages[] -> {name,badge,stops[]}. Each stop = one vertical slide: title, image (ONE; site photos /images/... , aerials + CABN photos media/...), caption?, lead, points[], layers[] (slugs shown), fit ('all'=whole property), cta?. 'Operating now' badge = green.

intro.title: Wells Gray — the Master Plan
intro.body:
393 acres of private land beside Wells Gray Provincial Park, near Clearwater, BC.

This guided tour walks through what's already built and operating, then the next phase of the plan — cabins along the creek, the prefab units, the festival grounds, infrastructure, and an art-and-nature trail network.

A dozen short stops. Use the buttons to move; close anytime to explore the map yourself.

stops.title: Built & Operating
stops.lead: Five seasons in — the working prototype the village builds on.
points: Full-service & power/water RV sites, plus rental cabins
Private swimming lake, shower houses & disc-golf course
Already drawing guests with minimal marketing

stops.title: The Sauna
stops.lead: A wood-fired cedar barrel sauna with a cold plunge.
points: Year-round — steam, then a cold dip by the lake
Anchors the wellness rhythm already running on site
A taste of the spa planned for a later phase

stops.title: The Geodesic Dome
stops.lead: A geodesic glamping dome for stays and gatherings.
points: Sleeps guests in comfort, open to the sky
Doubles as a space for movie nights and small events
One of the signature stays on the land

stops.title: The Event Gazebo
stops.lead: A 120-person event gazebo with a commercial kitchen.
points: Hosts weddings, gatherings and community meetups
Campfire and shared meals at the heart of it
The village's main indoor gathering space today

stops.title: Creekside Cabins
stops.lead: A small cabin community set along the creek.
points: Sited beside the water, a short walk from the campground core
Plugs into existing power, water, septic & internet — short spurs only
The first of the village's permanent homes

stops.title: The Prefab Cabins
stops.caption: Cabin model: CABN — MOR.II (cabn.co)
stops.lead: What the cabins will look like.
points: Built by CABN (cabn.co): BC-interior, net-zero, solar-ready
From the HUTT studio (~$39.5k) to the MOR.II (540 sqft, 1-bed)
CABN ships the timber shell; the maker space finishes the interiors
Delivered and craned onto prepared pads — fast, low-impact

stops.title: Inside the Cabins
stops.caption: CABN interior
stops.lead: Bright, efficient, built for the climate.
points: Lofted bedroom over a compact kitchen and living space
High-performance windows and an airtight, insulated envelope
Solar + battery options — off-grid-capable

stops.title: Festival Grounds
stops.lead: A leave-no-trace festival meadow — light-touch by design.
points: Clear the meadow and cut/clear the trails
Add a new shower house and grouped portapotties
Scales from 500 to 5,000 people, no permanent build
Cleared timber is milled on site for later-phase cabins

stops.title: Infrastructure Upgrades
stops.lead: Extending the services already on the land.
points: Power, water and septic trunked out from the existing campground systems
Starlink internet across the property
New developments tap in with short spurs — no new utilities from scratch
Sized to stay off-grid-capable and resilient

stops.title: Hiking & Art Trails
stops.lead: An art-and-nature hike threading the property together.
points: A cleared trail network through forest and along the creek
Art "landings" — installations and rest spots along the way
Links the campground, creekside cabins and festival meadow
Immersive nature, minutes from your door

stops.title: The Bigger Picture
stops.lead: Beyond these phases — a residential village, and a network.
points: A car-free wellness village, regenerative farm and lakeside spa are planned for later phases
Wells Gray is the flagship — a template for aligned villages elsewhere
